# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Pod specification for our container that needs to interact with the Docker socket for CI/CD operations. The pod needs elevated permissions to build and push Docker images. This runs in our build cluster for automated deployments.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: ci-cd-build-pod
  labels:
    app: ci-cd-build
spec:
  securityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    runAsUser: 1000
    runAsGroup: 2000
    fsGroup: 2000
    supplementalGroups: [1000]
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
    capabilities:
      drop:
        - ALL
      add:
        - CHOWN
        - SETGID
        - SETUID
  containers:
  - name: builder
    image: docker:20.10.21
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
      runAsNonRoot: true
      runAsUser: 1000
      runAsGroup: 2000
    volumeMounts:
    - name: docker-socket
      mountPath: /var/run/docker.sock
    - name: docker-root
      mountPath: /root/.docker
    - name: build-context
      mountPath: /workspace
    command: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
    args:
    - |
      set -e
      echo "Starting build process..."
      # Your build commands here
      echo "Build completed"
  volumes:
  - name: docker-socket
    hostPath:
      path: /var/run/docker.sock
      type: Socket
  - name: docker-root
    emptyDir: {}
  - name: build-context
    emptyDir: {}
  automountServiceAccountToken: false
  hostNetwork: false
  hostPID: false
  hostIPC: false
  restartPolicy: Never
